reply_text:
You clearly didn't watch it. They were on their way home on the last day when they became stranded... For four more days.

Tons of fruit? It would have been rotten and binned in no time in the heat and humidity. No refrigeration, no air conditioning, the temperature inside was meant to have been insufferable which is why they had no option but to live and sleep on the decks and make tents to help with the sun and heat.

Who would sunbathe?... They were stuck for nearly five days with every single toilet filled to the brim with poop. The urine and excrement were flooding the cabins and stinking out the ship, which comes with obvious health risks. They had no food that could be eaten, they were given a soggy bit of bread with a strip of rotten ham. Some people ran out of medication. There were children terrified because the ship was just drifting. 

You either just watched the trailer or watched the first five mins and assumed all it was was they were to poop in a bag and overreacting.

Laugh away tho.

reply_text: I'm a past crew member and left in 2017. Had tons of friends onboard when Covid hit, they were isolated to the guest cabins and meals delivered to them, no access to other crew members.. They were just running test after test and basically was inconclusive.
